columns.extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1, under 'Purpose' and 'Patients and Methods', states that 'Docetaxel added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)' was the add-on treatment. The 'Procedures' section on page 3 confirms that patients were assigned to 'ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m²'. Table 1, 2, and 3 on pages 3 and 6 also clearly label one arm as 'ADT Plus Docetaxel'.

plans.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The abstract and 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 state that the treatment arm involved 'ADT in combination with docetaxel'. Docetaxel is a cytotoxic chemotherapy agent.
The median age for the combination arm (treatment) is reported as 64 years in the 'Efficacy' section on page 4, first column, second paragraph.
The median age for the ADT alone arm (control) is reported as 63 years in the 'Efficacy' section on page 4, first column, second paragraph.

The control arm is identified as 'ADT alone' in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1. Figure 1 on page 2, Table 1 on page 3, Table 2 on page 6, and Table 3 on page 6 also refer to this arm as 'ADT Alone'.

The number of patients in the control arm (ADT alone) is 393, as indicated in the CONSORT diagram (Figure 1, page 2) under 'Randomly assigned to ADT alone (n = 393)'. This is consistent with the 'No. of Patients' for 'Overall' in the 'ADT Alone' column of Table 1 (page 3), Table 2 (page 6), and Table 3 (page 6).

The total number of participants in the CHAARTED trial is 790. This is stated in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1: 'In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned'. This is reiterated in the 'Results' section on page 2: 'Between July 2006 and December 2012, 790 eligible patients were enrolled (Fig 1)'. Figure 1 on page 2 shows 397 patients assigned to ADT plus docetaxel and 393 to ADT alone, totaling 790. Table 1 on page 3 also confirms these numbers under the 'Overall' subgroup.

The Gleason score distribution for the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel) is not reported in the PDF. I looked in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 2 for baseline characteristics and in Table 1 on page 3, which details patient subgroups, but no information on Gleason score was found.

The Gleason score distribution for the control arm (ADT alone) is not reported in the PDF. I looked in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 2 for baseline characteristics and in Table 1 on page 3, which details patient subgroups, but no information on Gleason score was found.

The Gleason score distribution for the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel) is not reported in the PDF. I looked in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 2 for baseline characteristics and in Table 1 on page 3, which details patient subgroups, but no information on Gleason score was found.

The Gleason score distribution for the control arm (ADT alone) is not reported in the PDF. I looked in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 2 for baseline characteristics and in Table 1 on page 3, which details patient subgroups, but no information on Gleason score was found.

The median follow-up duration is stated in the 'Results' section of the Abstract on page 1 as '53.7 months'. This value is consistently reiterated in the 'Efficacy' section on page 4 and the 'Discussion' section on page 5.

From the CONSORT diagram (Fig 1, page 2), 397 patients were randomly assigned to the ADT plus docetaxel arm. Out of these, 7 patients did not start treatment (5 due to patient withdrawal/refusal, 1 due to medical decision, 1 due to no treatment information submitted). Thus, 397 - 7 = 390 patients received docetaxel. The percentage is (390/397)*100 = 98.2%.
From the CONSORT diagram (Fig 1, page 2), patients in the control arm were assigned to receive ADT alone and did not receive docetaxel. Therefore, N = 0 (0%).

The PDF states that docetaxel was administered for 'up to six cycles' in the chemohormonal therapy arm (page 1, Abstract; page 2, Procedures). Tables 1, 2, and 3 (pages 3, 6) provide median OS, time to CRPC, and time to clinical progression, respectively, but not the median duration of treatment.

The PDF states that patients in the control arm received 'ADT alone' (page 1, Abstract; page 2, Procedures). While ADT is typically continuous, a specific median 'on-treatment duration' for ADT alone is not explicitly reported. Tables 1, 2, and 3 (pages 3, 6) provide median OS, time to CRPC, and time to clinical progression, respectively, but not the median duration of treatment.

The primary end point of the study was overall survival (OS). This is stated in the Abstract on page 1 and confirmed in the Procedures section on page 3.

The PDF mentions 'quality of life' in a general context on page 1 (Introduction) and page 6 (Discussion) as an outcome that therapies aim to improve. The 'Study Assessments' section on page 3 and 'Procedures' section on page 3 detail the endpoints and measurements, none of which include a Quality of Life Scale.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including '≥ four bone metastases with at least one outside of the vertebral column and pelvis' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods). However, it does not provide the specific N (%) for all bone metastases or the N (%) for bone metastases in the treatment arm separately from the high-volume definition. I looked in the abstract (page 1), Patients and Methods (page 2), Results (page 3-4), and all tables (Table 1, 2, 3) and figures (Figure 1, 2, 3, 4) but could not find this specific data.

The PDF defines 'high-volume disease' as including '≥ four bone metastases with at least one outside of the vertebral column and pelvis' (page 1, abstract; page 2, Patients and Methods). The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 and the 'Study Assessments' section on page 3 explicitly list the primary endpoint (overall survival) and secondary endpoints (time to development of CRPC, time to clinical progression, etc.). Quality of Life is not mentioned as an endpoint or reported outcome for the CHAARTED trial in these sections, nor in any of the tables (Table 1, 2, 3) or figures (Fig 1, 2, 3, 4). While the introduction on page 1 mentions that combining novel therapies 'improve quality of life and OS' as a general strategy, it does not state that QoL was an outcome measured or reported in this specific trial.

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The text on page 4 states: "289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm ... had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease." 'De novo metastatic disease' is interpreted as 'Synchronous' and 'combination arm' as 'Treatment'. The total number of patients in the Treatment arm is 397 (Table 1, page 3). Thus, 289 (72.8%).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The text on page 4 states: "... versus 286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease." 'De novo metastatic disease' is interpreted as 'Synchronous' and 'ADT alone arm' as 'Control'. The total number of patients in the Control arm is 393 (Table 1, page 3). Thus, 286 (72.8%).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The 'Metachronous' group corresponds to patients with 'prior local therapy' (PLT). For the Treatment arm, the total number of patients is 397 (Table 1, page 3). The number of 'Synchronous' patients (de novo metastatic disease) is 289 (page 4, text). Therefore, 'Metachronous' patients = 397 - 289 = 108. The percentage is (108/397) * 100 = 27.2%. This is consistent with the sum of 'High volume/prior local therapy' (49) and 'Low volume/prior local therapy' (59) from Table 1 (page 3) for the ADT Plus Docetaxel arm (49 + 59 = 108).

columns.column_name: Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronous | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The 'Metachronous' group corresponds to patients with 'prior local therapy' (PLT). For the Control arm, the total number of patients is 393 (Table 1, page 3). The number of 'Synchronous' patients (de novo metastatic disease) is 286 (page 4, text). Therefore, 'Metachronous' patients = 393 - 286 = 107. The percentage is (107/393) * 100 = 27.2%. Note that the sum of 'High volume/prior local therapy' (42) and 'Low volume/prior local therapy' (64) from Table 1 (page 3) for the ADT Alone arm is 106, but the footnote for Table 1 indicates that 'One-study form not submitted for one patient so prior local therapy unknown', accounting for the 1-patient difference.

Extract the 'Median Time (95% Cl; months)' value for 'Overall' subgroup under 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' from Table 2 on page 6. The value is 19.4.
Extract the 'Median Time (95% Cl; months)' value for 'Overall' subgroup under 'ADT Alone' from Table 2 on page 6. The value is 11.7.

The secondary end points included time to development of CRPC (castration-resistant prostate cancer), defined either serologically or clinically from the time of random assignment until PSA progression, development of worsening symptoms, evidence of radiographic progression, or patient's deterioration as per investigator's opinion. This information is found in the 'Procedures' section on page 3. Page 5 also mentions that 'all the secondary end points were in favor of the combination arm'.

The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states: '790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone.' The treatment arms are 'ADT in combination with docetaxel' and 'ADT alone'.

The abstract on page 1 and the 'Procedures' section on page 2 state that patients were randomly assigned to receive 'ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m 2 for up to six cycles' or 'ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg/m 2 every 3 weeks for up to six cycles without daily prednisone'. Table 1 on page 3 also refers to this arm as 'ADT Plus Docetaxel'.

The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states that '790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned'. This is further confirmed in the 'Study Design and Participants' section on page 2, which states '790 eligible patients were enrolled (Fig 1)'. Figure 1 on page 2 shows 397 patients assigned to ADT plus docetaxel and 393 patients assigned to ADT alone, totaling 790. Table 1 on page 3 also lists 397 patients for the ADT Plus Docetaxel arm and 393 patients for the ADT Alone arm, summing to 790 total participants.

The number of patients in the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel) is 397. This is stated in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 2, confirmed by the CONSORT diagram (Figure 1) on page 2, and also listed in Table 1 (page 3), Table 2 (page 6), and Table 3 (page 6) under 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' and 'No. of Patients' for 'Overall'.

The treatment classes are 'ADT in combination with docetaxel' and 'ADT alone'. This information is found in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 and consistently used as column headers in Table 1 (page 3), Table 2 (page 6), and Table 3 (page 6).

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: The study reports outcomes for 'de novo' metastatic disease, which is synonymous with synchronous disease. This is evident in Table 1 (page 3) under 'High volume/de novo' and 'Low volume/de novo' subgroups, and in Figure 3 (page 4) panels C and D. The text on page 5 also discusses 'high-volume de novo metastatic disease'.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: The study reports outcomes for patients with 'prior local therapy' (PLT), which is synonymous with metachronous disease. This is evident in Table 1 (page 3) under 'High volume/prior local therapy' and 'Low volume/prior local therapy' subgroups, and in Figure 3 (page 4) panels E and F. The text on page 5 also discusses 'high-volume disease with PLT'.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: The study explicitly defines and reports outcomes for 'high-volume disease'. The definition is on page 1 in the 'Patients and Methods' section. Outcomes are reported in Table 1 (page 3) under 'High volume' and 'High volume/de novo' and 'High volume/prior local therapy' subgroups, and in Figure 3 (page 4) panels A, C, and E. Further discussion is on page 5.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: The study explicitly defines and reports outcomes for 'low-volume disease'. The definition is implied by contrast with high-volume disease on page 1 in the 'Patients and Methods' section. Outcomes are reported in Table 1 (page 3) under 'Low volume' and 'Low volume/de novo' and 'Low volume/prior local therapy' subgroups, and in Figure 3 (page 4) panels B, D, and F. Further discussion is on page 5.

The study randomly assigned patients to receive either 'ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles' or 'ADT alone'. This is stated in the 'Patients and Methods' section of the abstract on page 1 and further detailed in the 'Procedures' section on page 3. Table 1 on page 3 also lists the arms as 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone'.

The trial name 'CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer)' is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1 and further discussed in the 'Patients and Methods' section on page 2.

The document provides median OS (Overall: 57.6 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 on page 3 and Figure 2 on page 3) but does not report OS Rate (%) at any specific time point for the overall treatment group.

The document provides median OS (Overall: 47.2 months for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 on page 3 and Figure 2 on page 3) but does not report OS Rate (%) at any specific time point for the overall control group.

The term 'Synchronous' is not explicitly used with OS Rate (%). The document refers to 'de novo metastatic patients' (which is synonymous with synchronous disease) and provides median OS for high-volume de novo (48.0 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 and Figure 3C) and low-volume de novo (58.3 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 and Figure 3D), but not OS Rate (%) at a specific time point.

The term 'Synchronous' is not explicitly used with OS Rate (%). The document refers to 'de novo metastatic patients' (which is synonymous with synchronous disease) and provides median OS for high-volume de novo (33.1 months for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 and Figure 3C) and low-volume de novo (59.8 months for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 and Figure 3D), but not OS Rate (%) at a specific time point.

The term 'Metachronous' is not explicitly used with OS Rate (%). The document refers to 'prior local therapy' patients (which is synonymous with metachronous disease) and provides median OS for high-volume prior local therapy (66.9 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 and Figure 3E) and low-volume prior local therapy (69.6 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 and Figure 3F), but not OS Rate (%) at a specific time point.

The term 'Metachronous' is not explicitly used with OS Rate (%). The document refers to 'prior local therapy' patients (which is synonymous with metachronous disease) and provides median OS for high-volume prior local therapy (51.7 months for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 and Figure 3E) and low-volume prior local therapy (NR for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 and Figure 3F), but not OS Rate (%) at a specific time point.

The document provides median OS (High volume: 51.2 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 on page 3 and Figure 3A on page 4) but does not report OS Rate (%) at any specific time point for the high-volume treatment group.

The document provides median OS (High volume: 34.4 months for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 on page 3 and Figure 3A on page 4) but does not report OS Rate (%) at any specific time point for the high-volume control group.

The document provides median OS (Low volume: 63.5 months for ADT Plus Docetaxel arm in Table 1 on page 3 and Figure 3B on page 4) but does not report OS Rate (%) at any specific time point for the low-volume treatment group.

The document provides median OS (Low volume: NR for ADT Alone arm in Table 1 on page 3 and Figure 3B on page 4) but does not report OS Rate (%) at any specific time point for the low-volume control group.

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients (N) for 'High volume' in the 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' (Treatment) arm is 263, found in Table 1 on page 3. The percentage (%) is 66.2%, found in the text on page 4: '263 patients (66.2%) in the combination arm ... had high-volume disease.'

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients (N) for 'High volume' in the 'ADT Alone' (Control) arm is 250, found in Table 1 on page 3. The percentage (%) is 63.6%, found in the text on page 4: '250 patients (63.6%) in the ADT alone arm had high-volume disease.'

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number of patients (N) for 'Low volume' in the 'ADT Alone' (Control) arm is 143, found in Table 1 on page 3. The total number of patients in the 'ADT Alone' arm is 393 (Table 1, 'Overall' row, page 3). The percentage is calculated as (143 / 393) * 100 = 36.4%.
